reason: The text presents God (through Moses at the burning bush) responding to the Israelites' suffering in Egypt with compassion, not blame. God explicitly acknowledges hearing the people's cry, knowing their sorrows, and understanding the oppression caused by their Egyptian taskmasters (Exodus 3:7-8). The passage attributes the people's predicament to Egyptian oppression, not to any fault of their own. No Hebrew leaders appear in this passage to blame the people; instead, God promises deliverance and instructs Moses to lead them to freedom.
